324.reason: [Error: This line resets lineNumber to 0 every time clearToken() is called, causing the line number to be lost and error reporting to use line 0 instead of the actual line number.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: This line resets lineNumber to 0 every time clearToken() is called, causing the line number to be lost and error reporting to use line 0 instead of the actual line number.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: This resets lineNumber to 0 every time clearToken() is called, causing the line number to be lost. The line number should be preserved across tokens, not reset.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  不应重置行号，否则后续错误会使用错误的lineNumber值
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: resetting lineNumber causes loss of line count, leading to incorrect error line numbers]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Resets the line number to 0 on every token read, causing all error reports to output line 0 instead of the actual line number.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The lineNumber variable is incorrectly reset to 0 every time clearToken() is called at the start of processing a new token. This causes the lexer to lose track of the actual source line number, resulting in incorrect error line numbers (e.g., 0 or 1) being reported in error.txt instead of the true line where the lexical error occurred.]

333.reason: Moves the read position back to the erroneous character, causing an infinite loop or repeated error entries when next() is called again.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Moves the read position back to the erroneous character, causing an infinite loop or repeated error entries when next() is called again.

299.reason: [The error category code is hardcoded as "a", but the lineNumber used here may be incorrect because when encountering a single '&' or '|' at the end of a line, the lexer might have already advanced to the next line before detecting the error. Additionally, in the case where the erroneous symbol appears on a new line that was just read, lineNumber is incremented before processing, so it reflects the correct line. However, the real issue is that when an error is detected inside next(), the current lineNumber is used, which is correct. The actual bug is that after calling error(), the lexer does not stop processing and may overwrite errinfo or continue incorrectly. But more critically: when a single '&' or '|' appears at the very end of file (i.e., no following character), getchar() sets currentchar=0, then backtrack() decrements currentPos, but if currentPos was already at end, this leads to negative index or inconsistent state. However, the primary visible error is that the error message always outputs error code "a", which is correct per spec, BUT the lineNumber might be off due to when lineNumber is incremented. Looking closer: lineNumber is incremented only when reading a new line via scanner.nextLine(). When processing a token on a line, lineNumber holds the correct line number. So that's okay. The real bug is in the handling of single '&' or '|' — the error is correctly flagged, but note that after setting type=Type.ERR, the main loop continues calling next(), which may clear or overwrite state. However, the critical flaw is in the clearToken() method: it resets lineNumber to 0 every time before reading a new token! This causes the error reporting to use lineNumber=0 instead of the actual line number.]

This causes the error reporting to use lineNumber=0 instead of the actual line number.]
